name: Fluconazole
executiveSummary: Urinary candidiasis. Superficial mycoses and deep infections in the non-neutropenic patient.
indications:
## Main indications

Urinary candidiasis. Superficial mycoses and deep infections in the non-neutropenic patient.

> Adjust according to institutional protocol and medical prescription.

adulto.presentation: Sachet containing 200 mg (Fluconazol Norgreen, Rivero, Fluconovag, Braun).
adulto.reconstitution: No prior reconstitution required. Conc: 2 mg/mL.
adulto.administration: Direct IV: No. Intermittent IV: Yes. Administer sachet contents over 60 min.
adulto.notes: Keep at room temperature and protected from light. Do not remove protective cover (black plastic bag) until time of use.

pediatrico.presentation: Tablets: 100-200 mg; Suspension: 10 mg/mL; 100 mL ready-to-use solution: 2 mg/mL
pediatrico.dose: Children: PO, IV: Esophageal and oropharyngeal candidiasis: 3-6 mg/kg/day, maximum dose: 200 mg/day; Systemic candidiasis and cryptococcal meningitis: 6-12 mg/kg/day every 24 h, maximum dose 400 mg; Adults: 200-800 mg/day according to severity of infection.
pediatrico.compatibility: Do not administer concomitantly with ampicillin, calcium gluconate, ceftazidime, cefotaxime, cefuroxime, ceftriaxone, clindamycin, furosemide, imipenem, and piperacillin.
pediatrico.notes: Fluconazole may increase serum levels of: cyclosporine, phenytoin, tacrolimus, midazolam, zidovudine, warfarin, cisapride. Rifampin decreases serum fluconazole concentration. IV administration: doses ≥ 6 mg/kg infuse over at least 2 h. Good CSF penetration. Elimination: 60% renal; adjust dose in renal insufficiency. See preliminary guide for prevention of medication-related teratogenesis.

neonatal.dose: Prophylaxis/treatment per NICU protocol (e.g. 6 mg/kg/dose every 72 h in preterm infants).

stability:
## Reconstituted (in vial)

- Not applicable.

## Diluted solution (for administration)

- Not specified.

## Pediatric guide

- Discard once opened.
adverseEffects:
## Adverse effects

Nausea, abdominal pain, diarrhea, reversible alopecia, nephrotoxicity, hypokalemia, rash (monitor), pruritus, anorexia, hepatotoxicity (rare), eosinophilia, leukopenia, thrombocytopenia, neutropenia.
